chunk_summary: This section focuses on a tele-checklist for monitoring adherence and relapse risk in celiac disease, leveraging digital tools for chronic disease management and neurodisability care.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of tele-checklists in managing celiac disease adherence and relapse risk.
Identify key guidelines for celiac disease management from NICE NG20 and ESPGHAN.
Explore the integration of digital tools in chronic disease and neurodisability care.
Recognize the importance of monitoring faltering growth in children with celiac disease.
key_facts: Celiac disease management is guided by NICE NG20, emphasizing recognition, assessment, and adherence to a gluten-free diet.
The ESPGHAN guide highlights pediatric-specific recommendations for celiac disease care.
Faltering growth is a critical indicator in children with celiac disease, as outlined in NICE NG75.
Digital tools can enhance patient adherence and reduce relapse risk in chronic disease management.
Tele-checklists provide structured monitoring for patients with celiac disease, ensuring compliance with dietary and medical guidelines.
reference_quote: "Coeliac disease: recognition, assessment and management (NICE NG20) — European Society for Paediatric Gastroenterology, Hepatology and Nutrition advice."
document_key_topics: Digital tools in chronic disease care
